name: Fried Pork Chops
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim the excess fat from the edges of the pork chops. Generously sprinkle them with salt and pepper.
2. Season the flour generously with salt and pepper. Dredge the pork chops in flour until coated on all sides. Shake off any excess flour. Pour enough vegetable oil into a heavy deep skillet (cast-iron is perfect) to fill it to 1 inch. Heat over medium heat to 375°F. A small cube of bread dropped in the oil will brown in about 30 seconds.
3. Add only as many pork chops to the skillet as will fit without touching. Cook them, turning once, until cooked through (no trace of pink remaining in the center) and deep golden brown, about 12 minutes. Use a long-handled fork or tongs to prevent getting splattered.
4. Remove the pork chops from the skillet to drain. Sprinkle with paprika and serve hot.
